title: Lab: Repair a Restricted Volunteer Notice
summary: Lab: Repair a Restricted Volunteer Notice retrieves prior skills in an original unfamiliar clipping stacking and transformed-label incident scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css overflow, css sizing constraints, css transforms, css filters, css shadows depth, css containment effects, css effects debugging in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real unfamiliar clipping stacking and transformed-label incident artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

options.text: Unfamiliar clipping stacking and transformed-label incident case 1: apply overflow hidden to the body so no component can cause horizontal scrolling.

Unfamiliar clipping stacking and transformed-label incident case 1: identify the overflowing content and sizing cause, preserve access, and use auto, scroll, clip, hidden, or visible only for an intentional contract.
Unfamiliar clipping stacking and transformed-label incident case 1: use overflow clip on every focusable card because clipped focus is still keyboard reachable.

options.text: Unfamiliar clipping stacking and transformed-label incident case 3: fixed height is the most reliable way to keep cards aligned.

Unfamiliar clipping stacking and transformed-label incident case 3: use intrinsic sizing and min or max constraints, allow content-driven block growth, and test extreme content before adding fixed dimensions.
Unfamiliar clipping stacking and transformed-label incident case 3: use max-content for every narrow-screen control because intrinsic width always fits the viewport.

options.text: Unfamiliar clipping stacking and transformed-label incident case 13: z-index values compare globally across every element on the page.
